member.name: James Naish
member.synopsis: James Naish is the Labour MP for Rushcliffe , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Academies
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has assessed the effectiveness of the academy trust governance framework in ensuring that community and family engagement is prioritised by academy trusts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Academy trusts should have reserved places for parents, carers or other individuals with parental responsibility within their governance structure. Single academy trusts should have at least two such places on the trust board. Multi-academy trusts should ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Academies: Parents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has issued guidance on the length of time parental representation can be excluded within academy trusts where a school is underperforming.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Academy trusts should have reserved places for parents, carers or other individuals with parental responsibility within their governance structure. Single academy trusts should have at least two such places on the trust board. Multi-academy trusts should ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Academies: Parents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of parental representation and involvement in governance arrangements within academy trusts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Academy trusts should have reserved places for parents, carers or other individuals with parental responsibility within their governance structure. Single academy trusts should have at least two such places on the trust board. Multi-academy trusts should ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Religious Buildings: Hinduism
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to support the security of Hindu temples and events.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that Hindu communities, and all faith communities, are able to worship and gather in safety. Hindu temples are eligible to apply to the Places of Worship Protective Security Scheme, which provides funded protective ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Social Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what progress she has made on responding to the recommendations of the Law Commission's review of disabled children's social care.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In line with the protocol agreed between the Lord Chancellor and the Law Commission, the department provided an initial response to these recommendations within six months of publication. This will be followed by a full response within 12 months, which wi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Walls and Fences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to improve party wall agreements.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Successive governments have taken the view that the Party Wall Act 1996 meets its purpose of providing a framework to prevent and resolve disputes between neighbours regarding party walls. No review of the Act has been undertaken, nor is one planned.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Pensioners: Advocacy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what discussions he has had with the Pensions Commission on engaging pensioner advocacy groups.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Last year the Government revived the Pensions Commission to explore the adequacy, fairness and sustainability of the pensions system and consider how to improve retirement outcomes for future generations of retirees. The Pensions Commission is engaging wi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Government will be able to provide an update on waiting times for compensation for those affected by the infected blood scandal.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The delivery of compensation is a matter for the Infected Blood Compensation Authority (IBCA). The IBCA Framework Document, published in March 2025, sets out the timelines agreed between IBCA and Cabinet Office; namely for the bulk of infected people to b...

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps the Government is taking to ensure that people who suffered significant harm as a result of interferon treatment through contaminated blood products are compensated under the Infected Blood Compensati
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government accepted the Inquiry’s recommendation to introduce a new, higher level of award for infected people who were treated with interferon. The new Level 2B infection severity band will provide more core compensation through the Financial Loss an...

writtenQuestions.heading: Coeliac Disease: Gluten-free Foods
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his department is taking to support those with coeliac disease with rising costs of gluten-free products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the financial burden that a strict gluten‑free diet can place on people with coeliac disease.NHS England’s guidance on prescribing gluten-free foods sets out a national framework for primary care, and integrated care boards (ICBs...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: ME/CFS: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps the Government is taking to support research and funding for treatments for Myalgic Encephalomyelitis.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to funding high-quality research to understand the causes, consequences, and treatment of myalgic encephalomyelitis, also known as chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS).The National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Deposit Return Schemes: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her department are taking to ensure that the Deposit Return Scheme will be accessible for those with disabilities and limited mobility.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra has published an Equality Impact Assessment of the Deposit Return Scheme. Officials have worked with the Cabinet Office Disability Unit and the Government Equalities Office and with disability groups, including Disability Rights UK to learn from the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Tax Avoidance

writtenQuestions.answerText: At Budget 2024, the Government committed to a new independent review of the Loan Charge. The purpose of the review was to bring the matter to a close for people who have not settled and paid their Loan Charge liabilities. The Review was published at Budge...

writtenQuestions.answerText: At Budget 2024, the Government committed to a new independent review of the Loan Charge. The purpose of the review was to bring the matter to a close for people who have not settled and paid their Loan Charge liabilities. The Review was published at Budge...

writtenQuestions.heading: Biodiversity: Property Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what guidance her Department plans to issue to developers and local planning authorities on the transitional arrangements for the revised Biodiversity Net Gain rules expected on 31 Jul
writtenQuestions.answerText: They will apply from the date of determination. So, applications after the date will be subject to amended regulations.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Biodiversity: Property Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether the revised Biodiversity Net Gain rules expected to come into effect on 31 July 2026 will apply to planning applications based on the date of submission or the date of determin
writtenQuestions.answerText: They will apply from the date of determination. So, applications after the date will be subject to amended regulations.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, a) what steps his Department is taking to ensure that the needs of women are reflected in national and local HIV strategies, policies and decision-making structures, b) what action his Department i
writtenQuestions.answerText: The HIV Action Plan for England, 2025 to 2030, sets out five core priorities needed to reach our ambition to end new HIV transmissions within England by 2030. One of these priorities is to address stigma and improve the quality of life for people living w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Software
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his department tracks current levels of NHS app usage across different patient groups.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We track current levels of NHS App usage through the NHS App Management Information monthly publication. This is broken down by integrated care board and region, rather than demographic or patient group.Future work will explore how to get more detailed da...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Radicalism: Universities
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of trends in the level of extremist activity on university campuses since October 2023.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to tackling those who spread views that promote violence and hatred against individuals and communities in our society, and that radicalise others into terrorism.We remain vigilant to the risk of extremist activity across all p...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Serving police officers have access to the 2015 police pension scheme, which provides life-long survivor benefits for spouses, civil partners and unmarried partners, including those who remarry or cohabit after losing a spouse. All eligible police officer...
